title: Acantholimon limbatum
taxonomy: Acantholimon limbatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This family consists of about 32 genera and more than 900 species of plants. Acantholimon limbatum is a type of succulent plant, which stores water in its leaves to survive in dry environments. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Iran and other parts of the Middle East. Acantholimon limbatum is a relatively rare and endangered plant due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Acantholimon limbatum, also known as a species of cushion-forming shrub, is a plant that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Acantholimon limbatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
